symptom: AKS cluster health probe mode (Shared/ServiceNodePort) not working: LB doesn't distribute traffic, reports unhealthy nodes, health-probe-proxy sidecar crashes
rootCause: Common causes: (1) Basic LB SKU used instead of Standard, (2) Feature not registered on subscription, (3) K8s version < 1.28.0, (4) Feature toggle off
solution: Use Standard LB SKU; register feature via 'az feature register --name EnableSLBSharedHealthProbePreview --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService'; upgrade to K8s >= 1.28; contact AKS team if toggle is off; verify cloud-node-manager has health-probe-proxy sidecar

symptom: High memory (working set) on disk-intensive pods, OOMKilled despite low actual application memory usage; significant difference between working set metric and actual app memory
rootCause: Linux kernel page cache (slab memory) inflates cgroup memory.current metric. working_set = memory.current - inactive_file. Disk-intensive apps cause kernel to cache filesystem data in slab_reclaimable, which counts toward pod memory usage
solution: Set realistic memory requests and limits in pod spec. Verify by: kubectl exec into pod → cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.stat → check slab vs anon ratio. Diagnostic: drop kernel cache on debug pod 'echo 1 > /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches' (non-prod only) to confirm

symptom: Node auto-repair fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError during reboot/reimage/redeploy
rootCause: VM extensions failed to provision on the node during auto-repair process
solution: Check VM extension error details in Azure portal: Resource groups → cluster RG → node → Extensions. See error code ERR_VHD_FILE_NOT_FOUND (124) for common cases. If persistent, contact Azure support

symptom: Node auto-repair fails with OutboundConnectivityNotEnabledOnVMSS error
rootCause: Node or VMSS has no outbound internet access configured, required for auto-repair operations
solution: Enable outbound connectivity for the VMSS using NAT Gateway, Load Balancer outbound rules, or user-defined routes. See VMSS outbound connectivity documentation

symptom: Node auto-repair fails with TooManyVMRedeploymentRequests error
rootCause: Cluster exceeded VM redeployment request rate limit; redeploy action cannot repair the node
solution: Manual troubleshooting of Node NotReady issue required. Follow basic Node Not Ready troubleshooting guide. If persistent, contact Azure support

symptom: All AKS cluster nodes change to NotReady status simultaneously after network-level changes (DNS changes, firewall rule changes, NSG additions, route table modifications)
rootCause: Network-level changes (DNS, firewall ports/FQDNs, NSG rules, route table for AKS traffic) break kubelet heartbeat communication with API server
solution: Verify and correct network-level changes; check connectivity to AKS outbound requirements using curl/telnet from node; stop and restart affected nodes; review AKS diagnostics for SNAT failures or IOPS issues

symptom: Node NotReady with pthread_create failed: Resource temporarily unavailable in syslog; containerd/kubelet thread exhaustion
rootCause: PID/thread exhaustion on node - application spawns too many threads exceeding default PID limit (32768). kubelet and containerd fail to allocate new threads
solution: Identify offending app consuming excessive PIDs; monitor thread count per cgroup; configure --pod-max-pids in kubelet; consider PID-based eviction; increase VM size as temp mitigation

symptom: AKS node NotReady with CSE provisioning failure; kubelet fails to start during node provisioning
rootCause: Custom DNS server misconfiguration - cannot resolve API server FQDN; missing conditional forwarders to Azure DNS; private DNS zone not linked to custom DNS VNet
solution: Configure custom DNS with conditional forwarders to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16); link private AKS DNS zone to custom DNS VNets; verify via az vmss run-command invoke with telnet/nslookup

symptom: AKS node NotReady with CSE failure due to API server network timeout
rootCause: API server unreachable from node - NSG blocking port 443 egress, NVA/firewall blocking traffic, or API server throttling
solution: Check AKS subnet NSG allows port 443 egress; check node-level NSG; verify NVA/firewall allows port 443; for VMSS: reimage or delete+scale up; for throttling: upgrade service tier

symptom: AKS node enters NotReady state due to expired certificates
rootCause: Kubernetes cluster certificates (apiserver.crt) expired, preventing kubelet API server communication
solution: Check cert expiry via openssl x509 -enddate; apply certificate auto rotation (no downtime); or manually rotate (requires downtime). Note: node image/same-version upgrade does NOT renew expired certs - only full CP+nodepool upgrade does

symptom: AKS node intermittently enters NotReady then automatically recovers; readiness probe failures
rootCause: (1) API server unavailability causing readiness probe failures; (2) VM host faults
solution: kubectl describe node for errors; verify API server: kubectl get apiservices; check network config; review resource usage via Container Insights; ensure proper service tier; reduce watch/get requests to API server

symptom: metrics-server pod OOMKilled in kube-system namespace
rootCause: metrics-server default resource allocation insufficient for cluster size/load
solution: Configure Metrics Server VPA to allocate extra resources

symptom: CoreDNS pod OOMKilled in kube-system due to DNS traffic spikes
rootCause: CoreDNS default scaling insufficient for DNS traffic volume
solution: Customize CoreDNS scaling; ensure system node pool has 3+ nodes; isolate user workloads to user node pools

symptom: User pod repeatedly OOMKilled (exit code 137); continuous restarts
rootCause: (1) Memory limits too low; (2) App memory leak; (3) User workloads on system node pool
solution: kubectl top pod or cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current; increase memory limits; move to user node pools; check memory leaks; verify Java/.NET runtime version for cgroup v2 after K8s 1.25 upgrade

symptom: Pod Pending: volume node affinity conflict
rootCause: PV nodeAffinity rules do not match available node labels; zone mismatch
solution: kubectl get pv -o yaml to check affinity; kubectl get nodes --show-labels; update PV nodeAffinity or add labels to nodes

symptom: Pod Pending: Insufficient CPU
rootCause: All nodes fully utilized or pod CPU request exceeds available CPU
solution: kubectl describe nodes; az aks nodepool scale; use larger VM sizes; optimize CPU requests/limits

symptom: Pod Pending: untolerated taint on nodes
rootCause: Node taints not tolerated by pod spec
solution: Check taints: kubectl get nodes -o json | jq .items[].spec.taints; add tolerations to pod spec; or remove taint

symptom: Cannot access AKS cluster API server: Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp x.x.x.x:443: i/o timeout when authorized IP ranges configured
rootCause: AKS cluster configured with --api-server-authorized-ip-ranges that does not include client IP address
solution: Add client IP to authorized IP ranges: az aks update --resource-group <rg> --name <cluster> --api-server-authorized-ip-ranges <clientIP>; for corporate networks check proxy/firewall egress IP

symptom: ACR image pull fails with 401 Unauthorized: failed to authorize: failed to fetch oauth token
rootCause: AKS kubelet identity missing AcrPull/ContainerRegistryRepositoryReader role on ACR; or service principal expired; or kubelet identity removed from VMSS
solution: Check role assignment: az role assignment list --scope <acr-resource-id>; attach ACR: az aks update --attach-acr <acr>; check SP expiry: az ad app credential list; verify kubelet identity on VMSS; reconcile: az aks update

symptom: ACR image pull fails with no match for platform in manifest or exec format error from az aks check-acr
rootCause: Container image architecture (ARM64 vs AMD64) does not match AKS node architecture
solution: Push images matching node architecture; or build and push multi-architecture images supporting both ARM64 and AMD64

symptom: ACR image pull fails with not found error: wrong registry name, repository, or tag
rootCause: Incorrect image reference - wrong registry name (e.g. azureacr.io vs azurecr.io), wrong repository name, or wrong tag
solution: Verify full image reference: <registry>.azurecr.io/<repository>:<tag>; common mistake is azureacr.io instead of azurecr.io

symptom: ACR image pull fails with 403 Forbidden when ACR has network restrictions
rootCause: (1) ACR private endpoint DNS zone missing VNet link for AKS VNet; (2) ACR public access restricted and AKS LB IP not in allowed range
solution: (1) Add AKS VNet link to privatelink.azurecr.io DNS zone; (2) Add AKS LB public IP to ACR firewall: az acr network-rule add --name <acr> --ip-address <aks-lb-ip>

symptom: ACR image pull fails with dial tcp <acrprivateip>:443: i/o timeout when using private endpoint
rootCause: VNet peering not configured or disconnected between ACR private endpoint VNet and AKS VNet
solution: Configure VNet peering between both VNets; verify peering status is Connected; test TCP from AKS node: telnet <acr-private-ip> 443; for hub-spoke use Azure Firewall

symptom: ACR image pull fails with net/http: TLS handshake timeout
rootCause: Network virtual appliance (NVA) or firewall/proxy misconfiguration blocking or delaying TLS handshake to ACR
solution: Check NVA/firewall/proxy configuration; verify routes to ACR; update NVAs with validated routes

symptom: ACR image pull fails with pull QPS exceeded when multiple jobs pull same images simultaneously
rootCause: Kubelet default registryPullQPS rate limit exceeded by concurrent image pulls
solution: Change imagePullPolicy from Always to IfNotPresent in deployment YAML to prevent unnecessary pulls

symptom: Cannot view resources in Kubernetes resource viewer in Azure portal: Unable to reach the API server
rootCause: (1) Authorized IP ranges enabled but client IP not included; (2) AKS is private cluster and portal accessed from non-connected network
solution: (1) Add portal client IP to --api-server-authorized-ip-ranges; (2) Access Azure portal from network with connectivity to AKS subnet

symptom: Client IP cannot access AKS API server: dial tcp <API-SERVER-IP>:443: i/o timeout from kubectl or Azure portal
rootCause: API server authorized IP ranges enabled but client IP not in the allowed list
solution: Get client IP: curl https://ipinfo.io/ip; add to authorized ranges: az aks update --api-server-authorized-ip-ranges <clientIP>

symptom: kubectl fails: dial tcp [::1]:8080: connectex: No connection could be made or You must be logged in to the server
rootCause: Kubeconfig file missing, in wrong directory, expired, or corrupted; KUBECONFIG env var pointing to wrong location
solution: Run az aks get-credentials --resource-group <rg> --name <cluster> [--overwrite-existing]; verify KUBECONFIG env var; move config to ~/.kube/config

symptom: Custom NSG blocks traffic to AKS LoadBalancer service: timeout despite pod running correctly
rootCause: Custom NSG on AKS subnet missing inbound allow rule for service port; AKS only manages NSG at NIC level, not custom subnet-level NSG
solution: Add inbound allow rule for service port in custom subnet-level NSG; AKS auto-creates NIC-level NSG rules but does NOT modify custom subnet NSGs

symptom: DNS resolution fails from inside pod but works from worker node; CoreDNS randomly forwards to wrong upstream DNS server when multiple DNS servers configured in VNet
rootCause: VNet DNS settings mix custom DNS servers with Azure DNS (168.63.129.16). CoreDNS forward plugin uses random policy to select upstream server, so some queries go to Azure DNS which cannot resolve custom/private domains. Node resolver uses sequential policy and always hits the first (custom) DNS server.
solution: Remove Azure DNS (168.63.129.16) from VNet DNS settings. Keep only custom DNS servers in VNet config. Configure Azure DNS as forwarder in your custom DNS server instead.

symptom: 'Error from server: error dialing backend: dial tcp <nodeIP>:10250: i/o timeout' when running kubectl logs, kubectl exec, kubectl attach, or kubectl port-forward
rootCause: NSG or firewall rule blocks inbound TCP port 10250 on the node subnet, preventing API server from reaching kubelet API on the node
solution: Check NSG on node subnet for inbound rules blocking TCP port 10250. Add allow rule for API server to reach nodes on port 10250.

symptom: 'Error from server: error dialing backend: dial tcp' when running kubectl top pods/nodes; metrics-server or admission webhook service unreachable
rootCause: The Kubernetes service (e.g. metrics-server, admission webhook) backing the API request is failing — pods not running, endpoints stale, or service misconfigured
solution: Check the error message to identify the affected service. Verify pods, services, and endpoints for that service are healthy. Fix or restart the failing service pods.

symptom: 'Error from server: error dialing backend: dial tcp' for kubectl logs/exec/attach in clusters without API Server VNet Integration; konnectivity-agent pods not ready
rootCause: Konnectivity tunnel between API server and cluster nodes is broken. konnectivity-agent pods in kube-system are not running or not in ready state.
solution: Check konnectivity-agent pods in kube-system namespace. Delete the pods to force restart. Verify required outbound network rules for Konnectivity are not blocked.

symptom: kubectl commands fail or AKS cluster creation/node pool scaling errors after restricting egress traffic with firewall/NSG/appliance
rootCause: Egress restriction rules conflict with AKS required outbound network rules and FQDN rules. AKS outbound dependencies use FQDNs without static IPs, so IP-only NSG rules are insufficient.
solution: Verify configuration against AKS required outbound network and FQDN rules. Use Azure Firewall with application rules (FQDN-based) instead of NSG IP-only rules. Ensure all required FQDNs and ports are allowed.

symptom: InsufficientSubnetSize error during AKS cluster creation, node pool scaling, or upgrade
rootCause: Subnet CIDR has no available IP addresses for new nodes. Azure CNI requires IPs for both nodes and pods (nodes × max-pods). Kubenet and CNI Overlay require IPs for nodes only. Upgrade operations need extra buffer IPs for surge nodes.
solution: Create a new subnet with larger CIDR range. Create a new node pool on the new subnet. Drain and migrate workloads from old node pool. Delete the old node pool. Cannot resize existing subnet CIDR in-place.

symptom: Intermittent timeouts accessing application on AKS; some curl requests succeed (HTTP 200) while others timeout; pod shows CrashLoopBackOff with increasing restarts
rootCause: Container exceeds memory limits and gets OOMKilled (exit code 137, reason: OOMKilled). Kubernetes restarts the container but it fails again, causing intermittent availability.
solution: Remove memory limit temporarily to observe actual memory usage. Set appropriate memory limits based on observed usage. Investigate potential memory leaks in the application. Configure liveness/readiness probes for faster recovery.

symptom: az aks command invoke fails with Operation returned invalid status Not Found - command pod stays Pending due to node resource constraints, taints, or SchedulingDisabled nodes
rootCause: The command-<ID> pod created by az aks command invoke cannot be scheduled because nodes have insufficient resources, NotReady/SchedulingDisabled status, or untolerated taints
solution: Increase node pool size, remove pod-excluding constraints (taints), adjust resource requests/limits so the command pod can be scheduled and run

symptom: az aks command invoke fails with admission webhook validation.gatekeeper.sh denied the request - Azure Policy blocks command pod creation in aks-command namespace
rootCause: Azure Policy (Gatekeeper) enforces constraints (e.g. read-only root filesystem) that the command pod does not satisfy
solution: Exempt the aks-command namespace from the restrictive Azure Policy assignments via Namespace exclusions parameter, or adjust the policy to allow the command pod configuration

symptom: az aks command invoke fails with Error from server (Forbidden): cannot list resource - user lacks required RBAC roles for runCommand
rootCause: User does not have Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/runCommand/action and commandResults/read roles on the AKS cluster
solution: Assign Azure Kubernetes Service Cluster User Role or a custom role with runCommand/action and commandResults/read permissions to the user

symptom: az aks command invoke fails in Azure Cloud Shell with Failed to connect to MSI, Get Token request returned Response [400]
rootCause: Known issue in Azure Cloud Shell where MSI token acquisition fails for az aks command invoke
solution: Run az login first before running az aks command invoke in Cloud Shell, or run the command from a local machine/VM with Azure CLI installed

symptom: kubectl commands timeout repeatedly - konnectivity-agent (or legacy tunnelfront/aks-link) pods not running due to overutilized host nodes
rootCause: Pods responsible for node-to-control-plane communication (konnectivity-agent) are on nodes that are overly utilized or under stress, preventing tunnel establishment
solution: Move konnectivity-agent to dedicated system node pool; reduce node utilization; check with kubectl get pod -n kube-system -o wide and kubectl top node

symptom: kubectl commands timeout - required outbound ports, FQDNs, or IP addresses are blocked by firewall or NSG, breaking konnectivity-agent tunnel
rootCause: Firewall or NSG blocks required outbound traffic for AKS control plane communication (konnectivity-agent requires specific ports/FQDNs)
solution: Open all required ports, FQDNs, and IP addresses per AKS outbound rules documentation; verify with az aks egress-endpoints list

symptom: kubectl commands timeout - ALPN (Application-Layer Protocol Negotiation) TLS extension is blocked, preventing konnectivity-agent connection
rootCause: Network appliance or proxy blocks the ALPN TLS extension (RFC 7301) required by konnectivity-agent to establish tunnel with control plane
solution: Enable ALPN TLS extension on the network path; check intermediate proxies/firewalls for TLS inspection that strips ALPN

symptom: API server overloaded with TCP timeouts - application or third-party tool (e.g. Istio mixer) leaks watch connections or frequent GET calls to API server
rootCause: Client app creates new API server watch connections on each internal secret read (e.g. Istio mixer bug), accumulating connections that overload API server
solution: Use watches instead of frequent GET calls; fix application bugs that leak watch connections (e.g. upgrade Istio); monitor API server request rates

symptom: API server overloaded - too many Helm release revisions cause excessive ConfigMap objects and memory consumption on nodes
rootCause: Helm deployments with unlimited revision history (default) create large amounts of ConfigMap objects that consume node memory and cause API server usage spikes
solution: Set --history-max parameter to a reasonable value when running helm init (v2) or helm upgrade (v3) to limit stored revisions per release

symptom: DNS resolution from User node pool pods fails, or kubectl logs returns TLS handshake timeout - custom NSG blocks traffic between node pool subnets
rootCause: When node pools use different subnets with custom NSGs, inbound/outbound rules may block UDP 53 (DNS to CoreDNS) or TCP 10250 (kubelet) between subnets
solution: Configure custom NSGs to allow required inter-subnet traffic: UDP 53 for DNS, TCP 10250 for kubelet; AKS does not auto-update custom NSGs

symptom: UFW (Uncomplicated Firewall) enabled on AKS node blocks port 10250, causing tunnel/Konnectivity failures: kubectl logs, exec, port-forward fail with dial tcp <nodeIP>:10250: i/o timeout
rootCause: UFW is installed on Ubuntu-based AKS nodes by default (disabled). If manually enabled, it blocks all ports including 10250, preventing kubelet communication through the tunnel.
solution: Disable UFW via az vmss run-command invoke --scripts ufw disable or allow port 10250 specifically with ufw allow 10250. Re-create tunnel component pods after change.

symptom: iptables rule on AKS node blocks port 10250, causing tunnel/Konnectivity failures: kubectl logs, exec, port-forward return dial tcp <nodeIP>:10250: i/o timeout
rootCause: Custom iptables rules with DROP target on tcp destination port 10250 prevent kubelet communication from API server through the tunnel.
solution: Delete the blocking iptables rule: iptables --delete INPUT --jump DROP --protocol tcp --destination-port 10250. Verify with iptables --list --line-numbers. Re-create tunnel pods after.

symptom: Konnectivity agent performance degrades as cluster grows: increased timeouts for kubectl logs/exec, potential OOM kills on nodes hosting konnectivity-agent pods
rootCause: Fixed replica count of Konnectivity agents becomes bottleneck in large clusters. Insufficient memory allocation or misconfigured Cluster Proportional Autoscaler causes OOM kills.
solution: Use Cluster Proportional Autoscaler for konnectivity-agent (edit configmap konnectivity-agent-autoscaler in kube-system). Adjust resource requests/limits. Monitor OOM kills with kubectl get events.

symptom: Error from server (Forbidden): resource is forbidden: User cannot list resource in API group at the cluster scope when running kubectl commands on AKS cluster
rootCause: Microsoft Entra ID account lacks required RBAC permissions. Depends on cluster RBAC type: local K8s RBAC, K8s RBAC with AAD integration, or Azure RBAC.
solution: Check RBAC type: az aks show --query aadProfile.enableAzureRbac. For local RBAC: use --admin credentials. For K8s RBAC: create RoleBinding/ClusterRoleBinding or add user to AAD admin group. For Azure RBAC: assign AKS built-in roles.

symptom: 429 Too Many Requests errors on AKS cluster: OperationNotAllowed, TooManyRequests, requests to Azure APIs throttled causing cluster operations to fail
rootCause: Excessive HTTP calls to Azure Resource Manager from cluster autoscaler, cloud provider, or third-party tools exceed subscription quota limits.
solution: 1) Upgrade to K8s 1.18+ for better backoff. 2) Increase autoscaler scan interval. 3) Reconfigure third-party apps to reduce GET frequency. 4) Split clusters into different subscriptions/regions. Use AKS Diagnose and Solve > Azure Resource Request Throttling.

symptom: Quota exceeded error during AKS cluster creation, scale up, or upgrade at large scale (>500 nodes)
rootCause: Regional compute quotas exceeded for the subscription when provisioning additional VMs for large clusters.
solution: Create a support request in the subscription to increase regional compute quota for the corresponding VM resource type.

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade is very slow, taking hours for large clusters
rootCause: Default max-surge value is 1 (one new node at a time), causing sequential node replacement across the entire cluster.
solution: Increase max-surge per node pool: az aks nodepool update --max-surge 5. Check PDB settings. Avoid B-series VMs in system nodepool.

symptom: Internal LoadBalancer service creation slow or failing with timeout at >750 nodes
rootCause: Standard Load Balancer (SLB) backend pool updates are a known performance bottleneck when cluster has more than 750 nodes.
solution: Scale down cluster to <750 nodes to create internal LB, then scale back up. Use Node IP-based Load Balancer (AKS preview feature). Keep an internal LB service running before scaling.

symptom: Pods report increased memory usage after upgrading AKS cluster to Kubernetes 1.25+; kubectl top node shows higher memory; increased pod evictions and memory pressure
rootCause: cgroup v2 is default in K8s 1.25+ on AKS. cgroup v2 uses different memory accounting that reports kernel memory (page cache, slab) as part of container memory.
solution: If only higher reported memory without evictions, no action needed. For evictions: increase VM memory or pod limits. Update apps to cgroup v2 compatible versions (Java jdk8u372+/11.0.16+; .NET 5.0+; uber-go/automaxprocs v1.5.1+). Temporary: revert cgroup v1 via DaemonSet.

symptom: Memory saturation or OOM errors in pods after upgrading AKS to Kubernetes 1.25: Java/Node.js/.NET apps crash with OOMKilled, CPU throttling, increased memory pressure
rootCause: Applications using frameworks not compatible with cgroup v2 (Java <8u372, .NET <5.0, Node.js) cannot correctly detect memory limits under cgroup v2, leading to over-allocation.
solution: Upgrade app runtimes: Java to OpenJDK jdk8u372+/11.0.16+/15+, .NET to 5.0+. Update monitoring/security agents to cgroup v2 compatible versions. Increase pod memory limits. Temporary: revert cgroup v1 via DaemonSet.

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or scale fails with Unable to get log analytics workspace info or Unable to retrieve log analytics workspace error (StatusCode=404, ResourceGroupNotFound)
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace or its resource group was deleted without first disabling the monitoring addon on the AKS cluster.
solution: If <14 days since deletion: recover workspace with New-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace (recreate RG first if needed). If >14 days: disable monitoring addon with az aks disable-addons -a monitoring, then retry.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with AksCapacityHeavyUsage error: region has limited capacity
rootCause: Significant growth in demand for AKS in specific regions causes capacity limits; non-EA subscriptions are throttled first
solution: Try different region; deploy with different settings (public vs private cluster); use EA subscription; retry later as capacity is reclaimed

symptom: Application fails with 'argument list too long' error when kubelet tries to run executable
rootCause: (1) Arguments provided to executable are too long, or (2) too many services in namespace cause kubelet to inject excessive environment variables for service discovery
solution: (1) Shorten argument list; (2) Reduce active services, or set enableServiceLinks: false in PodSpec and use CoreDNS for service discovery instead

symptom: Cannot delete AKS cluster: PublicIPAddressCannotBeDeleted, InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted, or InUseNetworkSecurityGroupCannotBeDeleted errors
rootCause: Cluster is associated with subnet, NSG, or public IP still in use by other resources (LB, App Service plan, etc.)
solution: Remove public IP associations from LB; remove LB rules, health probes, backend pools; dissociate NSG from subnet; remove App Service plan resources connected to AKS VNET, then retry delete

symptom: Cannot delete AKS cluster: CannotDeleteLoadBalancerWithPrivateLinkService or PrivateLinkServiceWithPrivateEndpointConnectionsCannotBeDeleted error
rootCause: Private link service on internal LB has active private endpoint connections preventing deletion
solution: Delete all private endpoint connections first, then delete the private link service, then retry AKS cluster deletion

symptom: Cluster autoscaler fails with 'cannot scale cluster autoscaler enabled node pool'; kubectl get nodes returns no resources; all pods Pending
rootCause: (1) VMSS attached to cluster was deleted, (2) tags/properties modified in MC_ node resource group, (3) node resource group was deleted
solution: (1) Reconcile node pool via 'az aks nodepool update'; (2) Fix tags via 'az group update' with correct AKS-Managed tags; (3) Run 'az aks update -g -n' to reconcile to goal state

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or scale fails with 'Changing property imageReference is not allowed' error
rootCause: Tags or resource properties in MC_* node resource group were modified or deleted, breaking SLO
solution: Add a new node pool, then delete the affected node pool

symptom: Cluster autoscaler not scaling up or down with 'failed to fix node group sizes: failed to decrease vmss: attempt to delete existing nodes' error in logs
rootCause: Upstream cluster autoscaler race condition causes deadlock (kubernetes/autoscaler#6128)
solution: Disable and re-enable the cluster autoscaler to break the deadlock

symptom: AKS cluster update or upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed error: cannot link virtual network to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces
rootCause: Original private DNS zone was disassociated and a new zone with the same name was linked from a different resource group/subscription, causing conflict
solution: Remove the VNET link to the wrongly-placed private DNS zone, then run 'az aks update -n <cluster> -g <rg>' to reconcile

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with Status=429 Code=Throttled: The PutManagedClusterHandler.PUT request limit has been exceeded. Occurs when too many AKS API requests are sent within a short time window.
rootCause: AKS resource provider throttling limits exceeded for the subscription. Independent of ARM throttling, specific to AKS RP operations.
solution: Reduce request frequency: run LIST scripts less frequently, space out deployments or use different subscriptions, ensure each operation completes before starting another.

symptom: AKS cluster creation/upgrade fails with AADSTS7000222: BadRequest or InvalidClientSecret. The credentials in ServicePrincipalProfile were invalid or client secret keys are expired.
rootCause: Service principal client secret expired, incorrect credentials provided, or SP does not exist in the Entra ID tenant.
solution: Check SP expiration via az aks show + az ad app credential list. Reset existing SP credentials or create new SP. Update AKS cluster with new credentials via az aks update-credentials.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with CniDownloadTimeoutVMExtensionError (exit status=41) on Linux or WINDOWS_CSE_ERROR_DOWNLOAD_CNI_PACKAGE (exit code 35) on Windows. Nodes cannot download CNI binaries from acs-mirror.azureedge.net.
rootCause: Nodes cannot connect to acs-mirror.azureedge.net to download CNI libraries. Typically NVA blocking SSL, SSL certificate inspection, or firewall blocking required FQDN.
solution: Verify connectivity: curl -I https://acs-mirror.azureedge.net/cni/azure-vnet-cni-linux-amd64-v1.0.25.tgz. Ensure traffic allowed to acs-mirror.azureedge.net:443. Check firewall/NVA SSL inspection rules.

symptom: AKS cluster creation/start fails with K8SAPIServerConnFailVMExtensionError (exit status=51). Unable to establish connection from agents to Kubernetes API server on port 443.
rootCause: Cluster nodes cannot connect to API server pod. Firewall/egress filtering blocking cluster FQDN, or firewall outbound IP not in authorized IP ranges.
solution: Verify from node: nc -vz <cluster-fqdn> 443. If using egress filtering, allow traffic to cluster FQDN. If authorized IP ranges enabled, add firewall outbound IP to authorized list.

symptom: AKS cluster creation/start fails with K8SAPIServerDNSLookupFailVMExtensionError (exit status=52). Agents unable to resolve Kubernetes API server name. Custom DNS server likely misconfigured.
rootCause: Nodes cannot resolve cluster FQDN via DNS. Common in private clusters with custom DNS missing conditional forwarder to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16) or DNS zone not linked to VNet.
solution: Verify DNS: dig/nslookup <cluster-fqdn>. Ensure custom DNS forwards to 168.63.129.16. For private clusters verify DNS zone linked to VNet. Reconcile failed creation with az resource update.

symptom: AKS cluster creation/scale/upgrade fails with OutboundConnFailVMExtensionError (exit status=50). Unable to establish outbound connection from agents. Nodes cannot reach mcr.microsoft.com:443.
rootCause: CSE cannot establish outbound connectivity to MCR (mcr.microsoft.com:443). Blocked by firewall, proxy, NSG, AAAA record blocking, or private cluster missing Azure DNS upstream.
solution: Use Azure Virtual Network Verifier for connectivity analysis. SSH to node: nc -vz mcr.microsoft.com 443 and dig mcr.microsoft.com. Ensure all AKS required FQDNs/ports allowed. Private clusters: add 168.63.129.16 as upstream DNS.

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade fails with UpgradeFailed/PodDrainFailure: Drain node failed when evicting pod, Too Many Requests error. Caused by restrictive Pod Disruption Budget (PDB) with ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS=0.
rootCause: PDB policy too restrictive - minAvailable equals current replica count, ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS=0. Pods resist eviction during node drain, upgrade fails, cluster enters Failed state.
solution: Three options: (1) Adjust PDB so ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS >= 1. (2) Backup and delete PDB, upgrade, reapply. (3) Scale workload to 0, upgrade, scale back. Reconcile with az aks upgrade to same version.

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade fails with SubnetIsFull error: subnet does not have enough capacity for IP addresses. Scaling or upgrading node pool fails due to insufficient subnet IP space.
rootCause: Subnet IP address range exhausted - not enough free IPs to create new nodes during upgrade surge.
solution: Reduce cluster nodes to free IPs for upgrade. If not possible and VNet CIDR has room, add new node pool with unique larger subnet, switch original to system type, scale up new, scale down original.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with SubscriptionRequestsThrottled (Status=429): Number of requests for subscription exceeded the backend storage limit on Network/PublicIPAddresses
rootCause: Azure Resource Manager throttling limits exceeded for the subscription due to excessive GET/PUT requests to specific resource providers.
solution: Use a different subscription, reduce automated script frequency, scope scripts to query only necessary resources, or distribute users across subscriptions.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError exit status=65 (ERR_VHD_FILE_NOT_FOUND). vmssCSE extension enable failed.
rootCause: Connectivity issue between AKS cluster and required Azure endpoints (mcr.microsoft.com, acs-mirror.azureedge.net). Exit code 65 is a rare substitute for codes 50/51/52.
solution: Review outbound network and FQDN rules for AKS clusters. Ensure all required API services FQDNs are allowed.

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with LoadBalancerInUseByVirtualMachineScaleSet (409) or NetworkSecurityGroupInUseByVirtualMachineScaleSet (409)
rootCause: VMSS is still using the associated public IP address or NSG when cluster deletion is attempted.
solution: Remove all public IP addresses associated with Azure Load Balancer, or dissociate the NSG used by the subnet before retrying deletion.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails because service principal is not found or invalid. Validation fails when SP has not propagated across all Entra ID regions.
rootCause: Service principal was just created and has not propagated across all Microsoft Entra ID regions yet, or SP credentials are incorrect/missing.
solution: Use existing SP that has propagated, wait 5-10 min after SP creation, verify with az ad sp show, or use system-assigned managed identity (recommended).

symptom: After restarting a stopped AKS cluster, current node count is outside the autoscaler min/max range.
rootCause: Expected behavior: cluster starts with node count needed for current workloads, not autoscaler settings. Min/max only apply during subsequent scaling operations.
solution: No fix needed - by design. Cluster will eventually enter desired autoscaler range after scaling operations occur.

symptom: Windows Server node pools not upgraded from Gen1 to Gen2 VMs during AKS node image upgrade via az aks upgrade or az aks nodepool upgrade.
rootCause: By design, node image upgrade only updates image version, not VM generation. Creating Windows2022 node pool without Gen2-compatible VM size also results in Gen1.
solution: Create new Windows Server node pool with Gen2 VM size (--node-vm-size). For K8s <1.25, also set --os-sku Windows2022. Verify Gen2 support with az vm list-sizes.

symptom: OperationNotAllowed error when starting, upgrading, or scaling AKS cluster: Another operation is in progress. Managed cluster in Provisioning State (Updating/Starting).
rootCause: A previous cluster operation is still in progress or left the cluster in inconsistent state. AKS blocks concurrent operations on the same resource.
solution: Wait for current operation to finish, use az aks operation-abort, check cluster state with az aks show, or retry failed operation.

symptom: Error "tls: client offered only unsupported versions" when connecting to AKS API server from client computer or VM
rootCause: Client TLS version is outdated; AKS API server requires minimum TLS 1.2
solution: Upgrade client TLS version to 1.2 or later. Follow Microsoft guidance on enabling TLS 1.2 support in your environment.

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade fails with error indicating NSG rule involvement; cluster cannot download required resources
rootCause: Custom NSG rules on the MC_ resource group or subnet are blocking internet traffic required for cluster upgrade
solution: Run 'az network nsg list' to find NSG linked to cluster in MC_ resource group. Check rules with 'az network nsg rule list'. Remove rules blocking internet traffic, then re-run az aks upgrade to trigger reconciliation.

symptom: API server pods in CrashLoopBackOff with webhook call failures; error 'failed calling webhook' with broken pipe; Konnectivity pods cannot be created
rootCause: Custom admission webhook (e.g., Kyverno) with failurePolicy=Fail causes deadlock during API server bootstrap - webhook blocks API server object creation, preventing API server and Konnectivity pods from starting
solution: Delete the validating and mutating webhook configurations causing the deadlock. For Kyverno, follow Kyverno troubleshooting guide. Consider setting failurePolicy to Ignore for webhooks that may interfere with system components.

symptom: API server high latency, timeouts, etcd database size approaching 8GB limit; etcd handles >10000 objects of a type; kubectl commands slow or failing
rootCause: Objects continuously created without cleanup (completed jobs, failed pods, unused ConfigMaps/Secrets) causing etcd database bloat and performance degradation
solution: Check etcd size: kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_storage_db_total_size_in_bytes. Clean up: kubectl delete jobs --field-selector status.successful=1; kubectl delete pods --field-selector status.phase=Failed. Define resource quotas and TTL values.

symptom: High rate of HTTP 429 errors from API server; 'The server is currently unable to handle the request' for kubectl get commands; aks-managed-apiserver-guard FlowSchema detected
rootCause: AKS applied managed API server guard (aks-managed-apiserver-guard FlowSchema + PriorityLevelConfiguration) after frequent API server OOM events; non-system client requests throttled
solution: Identify unoptimized clients via kube-audit logs. Tune API call patterns. After fixing, delete guard: kubectl delete flowschema aks-managed-apiserver-guard; kubectl delete prioritylevelconfiguration aks-managed-apiserver-guard.

symptom: Certificate-related errors in containers running on Azure Linux (Mariner) nodes; container cannot find ca-certificates.crt at /etc/ssl/certs; symbolic link points to nowhere
rootCause: Azure Linux stores certificates differently from Ubuntu: /etc/ssl/certs is a symlink to /etc/pki/tls/certs. Containers mapping only /etc/ssl/certs get a broken symlink since /etc/pki is not mapped.
solution: Map /etc/pki as a hostPath volume with DirectoryOrCreate type in addition to /etc/ssl/certs. This works for both Ubuntu and Azure Linux hosts.

symptom: Error stating AzureLinux option not supported for OSSku parameter when deploying Azure Linux AKS cluster via Azure CLI
rootCause: Outdated Azure CLI version or outdated aks-preview extension does not recognize the AzureLinux OSSku value
solution: Upgrade Azure CLI: az upgrade. Upgrade aks-preview extension: az extension update --name aks-preview.

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or scale operation fails when cluster is in failed state due to insufficient CRP quota, SubnetIsFull, or PDB eviction failures
rootCause: Cluster entered failed state from prior quota/subnet/PDB issues. Operations cannot proceed on a failed-state cluster.
solution: For quota: scale back within quota, request increase, then retry. For subnet: see SubnetIsFull troubleshooting. For PDB: remove or adjust PDB so pods can drain. For deprecated APIs (1.26+): mitigate stopped upgrade due to deprecated APIs before retrying.

symptom: AKS cluster cannot simultaneously upgrade and scale - second operation fails while first is still running
rootCause: Each operation type must finish on the target resource before the next request runs. Concurrent upgrade+scale is not supported.
solution: Run az aks show to check ProvisioningState. If Upgrading: wait. If Failed: resolve per failed-state guidance. If Succeeded: retry the second operation.

symptom: Adding Windows node pool fails with WINDOWS_CSE_ERROR_CHECK_API_SERVER_CONNECTIVITY (exit code 5): Unable to establish connection from agents to Kubernetes API server
rootCause: Windows nodes cannot connect to the cluster API server. Common causes: NSG deny rules blocking API server, egress filtering by firewall, authorized IP ranges blocking firewall outbound IP.
solution: 1) SSH to node, run Test-NetConnection <cluster-fqdn> 443. 2) If False/Timeout, check NSG rules on VNet. 3) If using egress firewall, ensure traffic allowed to cluster FQDN. 4) If authorized IP ranges enabled, add firewall outbound IP to authorized ranges.

symptom: AKS upgrade blocked with K8sVersionNotSupported, OperationNotAllowed (cannot skip minor versions), or NodePoolMcVersionIncompatible (node pool >3 minor versions behind control plane)
rootCause: Target K8s version deprecated in region, attempting to skip minor versions, or version skew >3 between control plane and node pool.
solution: 1) az aks get-upgrades to check available paths. 2) Upgrade control plane + node pool together (az aks upgrade --kubernetes-version <version>). 3) If version too old, create new cluster on supported version and migrate workloads.

symptom: AKS operation fails with AKSOperationPreempted or AKSOperationPreemptedByDelete: operation interrupted by another in-progress operation
rootCause: A subsequent operation was issued before the in-progress operation completed, causing the original to be preempted.
solution: Wait for previous operation to complete (az aks operation show --operation-id <id>), or run abort to stop the previous operation. Then retry.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with AvailabilityZoneNotSupported: the requested zone is not supported for the VM SKU in the selected region
rootCause: The requested VM SKU has zone restrictions in the subscription. SKU may not be available in the specified availability zone.
solution: 1) az vm list-skus -l <location> --size <SKU> to check restrictions. 2) If restricted (reasonCode: NotAvailableForSubscription), request access via Azure region access request process. 3) Or select a different AZ/SKU.

symptom: AKS cluster update/upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed error. A virtual network cannot be linked to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces.
rootCause: Original private DNS zone was disassociated and replaced with a new zone having the same name but in a different resource group/subscription, causing a VNet link conflict.
solution: Remove the link between the AKS cluster VNet and the incorrect private DNS zone, then run az aks update to re-provision.

symptom: AKS private cluster create/update fails with CustomPrivateDNSZoneMissingPermissionError. Service principal or managed identity lacks read/write permissions on custom private DNS zone.
rootCause: The cluster managed identity or service principal does not have the Private DNS Zone Contributor role on the custom private DNS zone resource.
solution: Assign 'Private DNS Zone Contributor' role to the cluster control plane identity on the private DNS zone resource. Allow up to 60 minutes for permission propagation.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with DnsServiceIpOutOfServiceCidr. The DNS service IP is out of the range defined by service CIDR.
rootCause: The --dns-service-ip value is not within the --service-cidr range specified during cluster creation.
solution: Ensure the DNS service IP (--dns-service-ip) falls within the service CIDR (--service-cidr) range.

symptom: AKS cluster deletion fails with InUseRouteTableCannotBeDeleted. Route table is in use and cannot be deleted.
rootCause: The AKS cluster's associated route table is still linked to a subnet, preventing deletion.
solution: Dissociate the route table from the subnet first, then retry deleting the AKS cluster.

symptom: AKS cluster create/update fails with InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts. Allocated outbound ports exceed available SNAT ports given the number of nodes and outbound IPs.
rootCause: The combination of node count, outbound IPs, and allocated ports per node is not feasible. Each outbound IP provides 64,000 SNAT ports; total ports must accommodate all nodes including surge.
solution: Adjust the configuration: increase outbound IPs, reduce allocated ports per node, or reduce node count. Formula: 64000 / ports_per_node * num_outbound_IPs >= max_nodes (including surge).

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with InvalidParameter error. Covers multiple scenarios: VM size not available in region, reserved/invalid cluster names, invalid service principal, invalid VNet/subnet/route table.
rootCause: One or more creation parameters are invalid — VM SKU unavailable in region, cluster name conflicts with Azure reserved words (MICROSOFT/WINDOWS/LOGIN/XBOX/AZURE), invalid SP, or misconfigured network resources.
solution: Check VM SKU availability in region, verify service principal validity, validate network resources (VNet/subnet/route table), and avoid reserved cluster names. See aka.ms/aks-naming-rules.

symptom: AKS cluster create/update fails with InvalidResourceReference. Referenced resource (subnet, load balancer frontend IP) was not found or is in a different region.
rootCause: Two scenarios: (1) VNet and VMSS are in different regions causing mismatch; (2) Default outbound rule 'aksOutboundRule' on load balancer was manually modified, breaking frontendIP reference.
solution: Scenario 1: Ensure VNet and cluster are in the same region. Scenario 2: Rerun az aks update with --load-balancer-outbound-ips parameter specifying the correct public IP resource ID.

symptom: AKS cluster deletion blocked by CannotDeleteLoadBalancerWithPrivateLinkService or PrivateLinkServiceWithPrivateEndpointConnectionsCannotBeDeleted error.
rootCause: A private link service is associated with the cluster's internal load balancer, and the private link service has active private endpoint connections that must be removed first.
solution: Delete all private endpoint connections first, then delete the private link service, and finally retry deleting the AKS cluster.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with LinkedAuthorizationFailed: service principal has permission on the VNet but lacks permission on a linked resource (e.g., DDoS Protection Plan join/action)
rootCause: The cluster identity (service principal or managed identity) has permissions on the primary resource but lacks permissions on a linked/dependent resource referenced in the operation
solution: Grant the service principal/managed identity the required permission on the linked resource mentioned in the error message. Use Azure portal Role Assignments to assign the specific action (e.g., Microsoft.Network/ddosProtectionPlans/join/action) on the linked scope.

symptom: AKS cluster deployment fails with MissingSubscriptionRegistration: subscription is not registered to use namespace (e.g., Microsoft.OperationsManagement)
rootCause: Required resource provider is not registered for the subscription, or the API version/location is not supported for the resource type
solution: Register the missing resource provider namespace using Azure portal (Subscriptions → Resource providers) or Azure CLI: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.OperationsManagement. Replace namespace with the one shown in the error message.

symptom: AKS node pool upgrade fails with NodePoolMcVersionIncompatible: node pool version and control plane version are incompatible (minor version skew >2 or node pool version > control plane version)
rootCause: Kubernetes version skew policy violation: node pool minor version is more than 2 versions behind control plane, or node pool version is greater than control plane version
solution: 1) Check control plane version: az aks get-upgrades --resource-group <rg> --name <cluster> --output table. 2) Upgrade node pool to a version within 2 minor versions of control plane and not greater than it: az aks nodepool upgrade --resource-group <rg> --cluster-name <cluster> --name <pool> --kubernetes-version <valid-version>

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with OperationNotAllowed: exceeding approved VM family Cores quota, or PublicIPCountLimitReached: cannot create more than N public IP addresses in region
rootCause: Subscription has exhausted the compute cores quota for the specific VM SKU family or reached the public IP address limit in the region
solution: Request quota increase: Azure portal → Quotas → Microsoft.Compute → filter by region → edit the specific SKU record → submit new limit. For PublicIP: Subscriptions → Usage + quotas → Provider: Networking → find Public IP Addresses → create support request for increase. Alternatively, reprovision in another region/SKU.

symptom: AKS cluster creation, update, upgrade, or LoadBalancer service deployment fails with PublicIPCountLimitReached: reached maximum public IP addresses allowed for subscription in region
rootCause: Subscription has reached the maximum number of public IP addresses allowed in the region. Triggered by any operation that creates a Public IP address including creating/upgrading cluster or deploying Kubernetes Service with Public Load Balancer.
solution: Navigate to Azure portal → Subscriptions → Usage + quotas → Provider: Networking → filter by AKS cluster region → locate Public IP Addresses record → click 'Create a new support request' → specify new limit and submit. Retry operation after quota increase takes effect.

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with RequestDisallowedByPolicy: Resource was disallowed by policy. Subscription has Azure Policy blocking creation of public IPs, NSGs, route tables, or other resources required by AKS.
rootCause: Subscription administrators assigned Azure Policy restrictions that prevent creation of resources required by AKS cluster deployment (e.g., public IP addresses, NSGs, UDRs, route tables).
solution: 1. Identify the blocking policy from the error message (policy name is last segment of the id string). 2. Either update deployment to comply with the policy, or add a policy exemption. 3. After fixing, run 'az aks update -g MyResourceGroup -n MyManagedCluster' to reconcile the cluster from failed state.

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade fails with ServiceCidrOverlapExistingSubnetsCidr: The specified service CIDR conflicts with an existing subnet CIDR in the virtual network.
rootCause: The cluster's service CIDR (set at cluster creation, immutable) overlaps with a subnet CIDR that was later added to the same VNet. AKS validates this before upgrade and blocks the operation.
solution: Solution 1: Delete the overlapping subnet (if no resources attached). Solution 2: Adjust the overlapping subnet's address range. Solution 3: Redeploy the cluster with a different service CIDR (cannot change service CIDR post-creation).

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with ServicePrincipalValidationClientError: AADSTS7000215 Invalid client secret provided. The service principal secret is expired or incorrect.
rootCause: The service principal secret provided for AKS cluster authentication is invalid — either expired, incorrect (client secret ID used instead of value), or the SP itself is deleted.
solution: Solution 1: Reset SP credential via 'az ad sp credential reset --name <sp-id>'. For existing clusters, update with 'az aks update-credentials --reset-service-principal --client-secret <new-secret>'. Solution 2: Create a new SP via 'az ad sp create-for-rbac --role Contributor' and update cluster credentials.

symptom: AKS node pool creation fails with SubnetIsDelegated: Subnet cannot be used because it is a delegated subnet for another Azure service.
rootCause: The subnet specified for the new node pool has delegation enabled for a non-AKS Azure service (e.g., Azure SQL, Azure Databricks). AKS requires subnet delegation to be either absent or set to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters.
solution: 1. Check current delegation via 'az network vnet subnet show --query delegations'. 2. Remove non-AKS delegation: 'az network vnet subnet update --remove delegations 0'. 3. Add AKS delegation: 'az network vnet subnet update --delegations Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters'. 4. Retry node pool creation.

symptom: AKS cluster create/update/scale fails with SubnetWithExternalResourcesCannotBeUsedByOtherResources: Subnet contains external resources (service association links, delegations) and cannot be used by AKS.
rootCause: The subnet has external resources associated with it (service association links, subnet delegations from other Azure services), preventing AKS from using it. Azure enforces that subnets with external resources cannot be shared across certain resource types.
solution: Option 1: Create a new subnet and associate it with AKS. Option 2: Delete the external references (service association links, subnet delegations) from the existing subnet, then retry.

symptom: Deleting AKS cluster fails with HTTP 429 TooManyRequestsReceived / SubscriptionRequestsThrottled — subscription read request limit exceeded
rootCause: Subscription-level or tenant-level ARM throttling limits reached; too many concurrent requests to Azure Resource Manager
solution: Wait for the Retry-After period specified in the HTTP response header before retrying the delete operation; reduce concurrent ARM calls

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade fails with UnsatisfiablePDB error — PDB has maxUnavailable == 0, can't proceed with put operation
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budget (PDB) with maxUnavailable=0 blocks node drain operations required for cluster upgrade
solution: Set PDB maxUnavailable to 1 or greater, or backup/delete the PDB before upgrade and redeploy it after upgrade succeeds

symptom: AKS cluster create/upgrade/scale fails with VirtualNetworkNotInSucceededState — VNet is in Updating or Failed state
rootCause: AKS can only set ownership on a VNet when provisioningState is Succeeded; another operation is running or a previous operation left VNet in Failed state
solution: Check VNet provisioning state with 'az network vnet show --query provisioningState'; wait for concurrent operations to finish or fix failed VNet state, then retry

symptom: AKS Linux cluster creation fails with VMExtensionError_CniDownloadTimeout (ERR_CNI_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT) — unable to download CNI libraries
rootCause: Cluster nodes cannot reach acs-mirror.azureedge.net to download CNI binaries; typically blocked by NVA or SSL inspection
solution: Verify nodes can curl https://acs-mirror.azureedge.net/cni/azure-vnet-cni-linux-amd64-v1.0.25.tgz; ensure required FQDNs are allowed in firewall/NVA egress rules

symptom: AKS cluster start/create fails with VMExtensionError_K8SAPIServerConnFail (exit code 51) — agents unable to connect to Kubernetes API server
rootCause: Cluster nodes cannot establish TCP connection to API server on port 443; firewall or NSG blocking egress to cluster FQDN, or authorized IP ranges blocking firewall outbound IP
solution: Run 'nc -vz <cluster-fqdn> 443' from node to verify; allow traffic to cluster FQDN in firewall; add firewall outbound IP to authorized IP ranges if enabled

symptom: AKS cluster start/create fails with VMExtensionError_K8SAPIServerDNSLookupFail — agents unable to resolve Kubernetes API server name
rootCause: Custom DNS server misconfigured; private DNS zone not linked to VNet; conditional forwarding doesn't support subdomains
solution: Verify DNS resolution with dig/nslookup on node; link private DNS zone to VNet; configure custom DNS correctly for private cluster; reconcile cluster with 'az resource update' if needed

symptom: AKS cluster creation with outbound type none/block fails with OrasPullNetworkTimeoutVMExtensionError (exit code 211) — Bootstrap Container Registry not reachable
rootCause: Network isolated cluster's private ACR cache (bootstrap ACR) is unreachable due to incorrect private link or DNS zone configuration
solution: Verify ACR cache rule includes aks-managed-rule with source mcr.microsoft.com/*; check private link and private DNS zone config; SSH to node and curl ACR host; reconcile cluster after fix

symptom: AKS cluster creation with outbound type none/block fails with OrasPullUnauthorizedVMExtensionError (exit code 212) — Bootstrap Container Registry authorization failed
rootCause: Kubelet identity missing AcrPull or Container Registry Repository Reader role on bootstrap ACR; or identity not bound to VMSS; or IMDS connection timeout
solution: Assign AcrPull (or Container Registry Repository Reader for ABAC-enabled) role to kubelet identity on bootstrap ACR; manually bind identity to VMSS if not found; submit support ticket for IMDS timeout; reconcile cluster

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with VMExtensionProvisioningTimeout: VM extension provisioning timed out. Custom script extension cannot establish connectivity to download K8s/CNI binaries or resolve DNS
rootCause: CSE cannot reach endpoints for downloading Kubernetes/CNI binaries, outbound connectivity blocked, DNS resolution failure, or apt-get update timeout during node provisioning
solution: 1) Check egress filtering setup against AKS required FQDNs (aka.ms/aks/outbound-rules). 2) Verify DNS resolution for cluster FQDN on custom DNS. 3) For private clusters ensure Azure DNS 168.63.129.16 is upstream DNS. 4) Review firewall rules not blocking required ports

symptom: AKS cluster create/upgrade/scale fails with ZonalAllocationFailed: insufficient capacity for requested VM size in the zone
rootCause: Azure region/zone has insufficient capacity for the requested VM SKU. Can also be triggered by proximity placement group constraints
solution: 1) Use different VM SKU. 2) Deploy to different zone/region. 3) Use Node Auto Provisioning (NAP) for dynamic SKU selection. 4) For upgrades use MaxUnavailable instead of MaxSurge to avoid needing surge capacity. 5) Use cluster-autoscaler priority expander with fallback node pools

symptom: AKS cluster create/scale fails with OverconstrainedAllocationRequest: VM cannot be allocated due to too many constraints (ephemeral disk, accelerated networking, availability zone, PPG)
rootCause: Combination of VM constraints (size, SKU, accelerated networking, ephemeral disk, proximity placement group) is too restrictive for Azure to find matching capacity
solution: Create new node pool without proximity placement group association. Reduce constraints by removing ephemeral disk or accelerated networking requirements. For dedicated hosts, ensure enough hosts spanning required fault domains

symptom: AKS Cost Analysis add-on enable fails with InvalidDiskCSISettingForCostAnalysis or InvalidManagedIdentitySettingForCostAnalysis error
rootCause: Cost Analysis add-on requires Azure Disk CSI driver enabled AND managed identity (system or user-assigned). Missing either prerequisite causes enablement failure
solution: Enable Disk CSI driver: az aks update --enable-disk-driver. Enable managed identity: az aks update --enable-managed-identity. Also requires Standard or Premium tier (not free tier)

symptom: AKS Cost Analysis add-on cost-analysis-agent pod OOMKilled or stuck in Pending state on large clusters
rootCause: Memory limit is 4GB (supports ~7000 containers). Large clusters exceed this limit causing OOMKill. Pending state caused by insufficient allocatable memory on nodes (request is 500MB)
solution: For OOMKill: disable the add-on as custom memory limits not supported. For Pending: ensure nodes have 500MB+ allocatable memory. Memory usage formula: ~200MB + 0.5MB per container

symptom: KEDA 2.14/2.15 breaking changes cause ScaledObject failures after AKS K8s upgrade to 1.30+: pod-managed identity no longer supported, metricName in metadata deprecated, clientSecret removed for Azure Data Explorer scaler
rootCause: KEDA 2.14 (K8s 1.30) removes: metadata.clientSecret for ADX scaler, metricName from trigger metadata. KEDA 2.15 (K8s 1.32) removes pod-managed identity support entirely
solution: 1) Migrate pod-managed identity to workload identity. 2) Move metricName from trigger.metadata to trigger.name. 3) Replace metadata.clientSecret with clientSecretFromEnv for ADX scaler. 4) Test in non-prod before K8s upgrade. Check affected: kubectl get TriggerAuthentication --all-namespaces -o jsonpath for podIdentity azure provider

symptom: AKS cluster extension creation fails with 'Unable to get a response from the agent in time' - extension-agent/extension-operator pods not initialized due to resource limits, policy restrictions, or NoSchedule taints
rootCause: Cluster extension agent and manager pods fail to initialize because of resource limitations, policy restrictions, or node taints (NoSchedule). Pods stuck in unready state.
solution: Run kubectl describe pod -n kube-system extension-operator-{id} to identify scheduling issues. Ensure sufficient resources, remove blocking taints, adjust policies. For ARC clusters check azure-arc namespace.

symptom: AKS cluster extension agent returns 403 'This traffic is not authorized' when calling <region>.dp.kubernetesconfiguration.azure.com
rootCause: A preexisting PrivateLinkScope for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes shares the same VNet/private DNS with the AKS cluster, causing extension data plane traffic to route through private IP instead of public IP.
solution: Option 1 (recommended): Create separate VNets for Arc and AKS. Option 2: Create CoreDNS override to resolve extension DP endpoint to public IP via coredns-custom ConfigMap hosts plugin.

symptom: AKS extension pods stuck in Pending with 'untolerated taint CriticalAddonsOnly: true' - cannot schedule on any node pool
rootCause: All node pools in the cluster have CriticalAddonsOnly taint and extension pods lack the toleration, so they cannot be scheduled.
solution: Add a new node pool without CriticalAddonsOnly taint, or remove the taint from an existing pool. Do not use CriticalAddonsOnly on single node pool clusters.

symptom: AKS extension Helm chart install fails with 'Timed out waiting for resource readiness' or 'BackoffLimitExceeded'
rootCause: Common causes: insufficient CPU/memory, policy constraints or NoSchedule taints blocking pods, architecture mismatch (Linux app on Windows node), or incorrect Helm chart configuration values.
solution: Ensure sufficient cluster resources and proper node scheduling. Check namespace events for pod startup issues. Verify all Helm chart configuration values are correct and complete.

symptom: Custom Azure Policy for validating controllers fails on AKS cluster
rootCause: The custom policy definition uses incorrect syntax that prevents it from being properly evaluated.
solution: Use the Visual Studio Code extension for Azure Policy to generate the custom policy definition with correct syntax. Reference the Azure Policy for Kubernetes custom policy support guide.

symptom: AKS upgrade fails when blocked by Azure custom policy - portal shows compliant but CLI upgrade is rejected
rootCause: During AKS upgrade, the policies evaluated differ from those for a running cluster. CLI-triggered upgrades may hit different policy evaluation paths than the portal.
solution: Use Azure portal instead of Azure CLI to start the AKS upgrade. Review and adjust custom policies that may conflict with upgrade operations.

symptom: AKS Deployment Safeguards not taking effect - noncompliant resources deployed without warnings, compliance dashboard shows Not started or empty
rootCause: Azure Policy add-on not enabled on the cluster, or the target namespace is excluded from safeguards evaluation.
solution: Verify Azure Policy add-on is enabled: az aks show --query addonProfiles.azurepolicy. Check namespace exclusions: az aks safeguards show. To disable entirely: az aks safeguards delete.

symptom: AKS container deployment denied by Gatekeeper: 'container has no resource/memory limit' - CPU and memory limits not enforced
rootCause: Container spec missing CPU and memory resource limits, causing Azure Policy/Gatekeeper to deny the deployment.
solution: Add resources.requests and resources.limits (cpu/memory) to all container specs. Check compliance with: kubectl describe k8sazurecontainerlimits. Trigger on-demand rescan via Azure CLI after fixing.

symptom: Azure Policy reports running container (e.g. Gatekeeper) as ConstraintNotProcessed despite container actually running
rootCause: AKS cluster version incompatibility or outdated version causing Azure Policy constraint processing issues.
solution: Upgrade the AKS cluster to a newer supported version to resolve the constraint processing compatibility issue.

symptom: Terraform policy assignment fails with 'Policy definition not found' when using azurerm_policy_assignment for Kubernetes
rootCause: The azurerm_policy_set_definition resource is not yet created when the policy assignment tries to reference it - missing dependency declaration in Terraform.
solution: Add depends_on = [azurerm_policy_definition.policies] to the azurerm_policy_assignment resource block to ensure correct creation order.

symptom: azure-defender-publisher DaemonSet pods continually fail and restart after enabling Defender Profile for AKS via Azure Advisor recommendation
rootCause: Authentication issues or misconfiguration when Defender Profile is enabled alongside Azure Policy Add-on for AKS.
solution: Workaround: Disable Azure Defender profile via REST API PUT call setting securityProfile.azureDefender.enabled=false. Use az account get-access-token to get bearer token, then curl PUT to management.azure.com.

symptom: Istio CNI enabled but istio-init containers still injected into new pods, CNI not performing traffic redirection
rootCause: Istio CNI DaemonSet not properly provisioned - CNI plugin installation failures, network config errors, or node taints preventing CNI pod scheduling
solution: Verify CNI DaemonSet running on all nodes: kubectl get daemonset azure-service-mesh-istio-cni-addon-node -n aks-istio-system; check DaemonSet pod logs for errors; verify node taints/tolerations allow CNI pod scheduling

symptom: Istio CNI enabled but pods fail to start with connection refused error in istio-validation init container
rootCause: CNI traffic redirection setup failed - istio-validation container detects that iptables rules were not properly configured by CNI plugin
solution: Check istio-validation logs: kubectl logs POD -c istio-validation; verify CNI DaemonSet is healthy; check pod events for network setup failures

symptom: Istio add-on egress gateway cannot be used on cluster
rootCause: Static Egress Gateway is not supported on Azure CNI Pod Subnet clusters
solution: Use a different CNI configuration (not Azure CNI Pod Subnet); verify cluster CNI plugin type before enabling Istio egress gateway

symptom: Istio egress gateway pods stuck in ContainerCreating state
rootCause: StaticGatewayConfiguration does not have egressIpPrefix assigned yet; kube-egress-gateway-cni-manager blocks istio-proxy creation until IP prefix provisioned (up to 5 min)
solution: Check StaticGatewayConfiguration status for egressIpPrefix; wait up to 5 min; verify gatewayNodepoolName references valid Gateway mode agent pool; check kube-egress-gateway-cni-manager logs

symptom: Istio Gateway API ingress not receiving traffic - Azure Load Balancer health probes failing
rootCause: Default Azure LB health probes not configured for Istio Gateway API deployment; health probe path/port/protocol mismatch
solution: Add Azure LB annotations to Gateway spec: health-probe-request-path=/healthz/ready, probe_protocol=http, probe_port=15021; or customize via GatewayClass/Gateway ConfigMap

symptom: High memory consumption in Envoy sidecar proxies across Istio mesh
rootCause: Envoy statistics collection with high cardinality metrics; Istio adds all cluster services to every Envoy config by default
solution: Use Sidecar CRD to restrict egress scope; enable discoverySelectors in MeshConfig; review and reduce custom metrics cardinality

symptom: Application pod becomes unresponsive or restarts when Istio sidecar is injected - race condition
rootCause: Application container starts before Envoy sidecar proxy is ready, causing network requests to fail
solution: Set holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts: true in MeshConfig defaultConfig section

symptom: Istio add-on error: Istio based Azure service mesh is incompatible with feature
rootCause: Conflicting extension (e.g. Open Service Mesh) enabled alongside Istio add-on
solution: Disable the conflicting feature and clean up corresponding resources before enabling Istio add-on

symptom: Istio ingress gateway pod crashes or not ready
rootCause: Istiod control plane not ready (ingress gateway depends on istiod); or gateway selector not matching correct label
solution: Verify istiod pod ready first; set correct gateway selector: istio: aks-istio-ingressgateway-external or internal; check Helm releases in aks-istio-ingress namespace

symptom: Istio secure ingress gateway TLS not working - certificate secret not created
rootCause: Azure Key Vault access for managed identity of secrets provider add-on set up incorrectly; SecretProviderClass or sync pod failed
solution: Verify secrets in aks-istio-ingress namespace; check Key Vault access policy for managed identity; verify SecretProviderClass and secrets-store-sync pod

symptom: Istio MeshConfig changes not taking effect or rejected
rootCause: Editing default ConfigMap instead of shared ConfigMap; tab characters in YAML; fields not on allowlist or misspelled; wrong revision in ConfigMap name
solution: Edit istio-shared-configmap-<revision> not istio-<revision>; use spaces not tabs; verify field names match allowlist; ensure ConfigMap name matches current revision

symptom: Istio Envoy using all CPU cores - high CPU consumption
rootCause: MeshConfig concurrency field set to 0, causing Envoy to use all available CPU cores
solution: Remove concurrency from MeshConfig; if not configured, CPU requests/limits determine core count

symptom: Data plane workloads dropped from Istio mesh after minor revision upgrade - sidecars no longer injected
rootCause: Namespace revision labels not updated to match new control plane revision before completing/rolling back upgrade
solution: Relabel namespaces: kubectl label namespace <ns> istio.io/rev=asm-x-y --overwrite; restart deployments to trigger sidecar reinjection; verify sidecar images

symptom: az aks mesh get-upgrades returns no available Istio upgrades
rootCause: Next Istio revision incompatible with current AKS cluster Kubernetes version
solution: Run az aks mesh get-revisions to check compatibility; upgrade cluster version first if needed; cluster upgrade permitted even if incompatible with current mesh

symptom: Istio plug-in CA - istiod pods stuck in Init:0/2 state with 403 Forbidden error
rootCause: Azure Key Vault access incorrectly configured - managed identity missing get/list permissions on Key Vault secrets
solution: Grant permissions: az keyvault set-policy --object-id <MI-objectId> --secret-permissions get list; for RBAC model add Key Vault Reader role; then reinstall Istio add-on

symptom: Istio plug-in CA - istiod pods stuck in Init:0/2 with SecretNotFound 404 error mounting cacerts volume
rootCause: Certificate secret objects missing or misconfigured in Azure Key Vault
solution: Verify all 4 secret objects exist in Key Vault with correct names; ensure objects are of type Secret (not Certificate or Key)

symptom: Istio plug-in CA certificate changes in Key Vault not auto-detected by cluster
rootCause: Auto-rotation for Azure Key Vault secrets provider add-on not enabled
solution: Enable auto-rotation; check with az aks show --query addonProfiles.azureKeyvaultSecretsProvider.config.enableSecretRotation; default poll interval 2 min

symptom: Pods with system-cluster-critical or system-node-critical priority class names are unexpectedly accepted and created in user namespaces in AKS
rootCause: Expected behavior by design; system-critical priority classes already exist in the system and are allowed in user namespaces
solution: No action required; this is intentional and not a security risk. Review other cluster security aspects if concerned

symptom: Azure Policy failure alert shows empty list of affected resources in AKS cluster
rootCause: Delay or Graph API issue causing alert to display empty resource list even if violation is resolved
solution: Wait for system to auto-update alert status; if persists >1 week, open Azure Support case with policy and alert details

symptom: Azure Policy cannot exclude privileged containers from AKS clusters
rootCause: Azure built-in policies lack container exclusion parameters for AKS clusters
solution: Create an exemption in default Azure Security Center policy, or create a new policy assignment that excludes desired pods/containers

symptom: Third-party scanner (e.g. Prisma) detects run-time host incident alert in AKS
rootCause: Alert typically generated by the container file system, not necessarily a real threat
solution: Review the specific container causing the alert; verify container and components come from trusted sources; ensure no unauthorized access indicators

symptom: KAITO workspace not created after enabling AI Toolchain Operator add-on; validation webhook blocks CR
rootCause: Wrong values in KAITO workspace custom resource specification rejected by workspace controller validation webhook
solution: Check gpu-provisioner and workspace pod logs; ensure GPU VM size meets model requirements; track with kubectl get machine/workspace -o wide

symptom: GPU node not created by KAITO gpu-provisioner controller; machine CR shows quota error
rootCause: GPU quota limitations in subscription or region prevent node provisioning
solution: Request quota increase for GPU VM family via Azure portal; check GPU instance availability in region; consider switching region or GPU VM size

symptom: KAITO model inference image not pulled; resource ready condition not True after long wait
rootCause: Inference images are very large (30-100GB); private image access mode may block pull; networking setup affects pull speed
solution: Verify image access mode and pull secrets; expect longer pull times for large images (tens of minutes); check cluster networking configuration

symptom: Azure App Configuration extension installation fails with Resource already existing in cluster / helm ownership metadata error
rootCause: Azure App Configuration Kubernetes Provider already installed via helm install; CRD ownership metadata conflicts with extension installation
solution: Uninstall Azure App Configuration Kubernetes Provider first (clean up resources), then install the AKS extension

symptom: Azure App Configuration extension installation fails with Failed to resolve the extension version
rootCause: Specified extension version does not exist or is not a supported version
solution: Retry installation using a supported version of the Azure App Configuration extension per official docs

symptom: Azure App Configuration extension fails with Extension type microsoft.appconfiguration is not registered in region
rootCause: Azure App Configuration extension not available in the specified AKS region
solution: Install in a supported region that the cluster extension supports

symptom: Dapr extension installation fails with no visible error message; extension status shows InstallationFailed
rootCause: Wrong key used in configuration settings (e.g., global.ha=false instead of global.ha.enabled=false), or extension-immutable-values configMap not found
solution: Inspect error via az k8s-extension list --cluster-type managedClusters; fix config key names; restart cluster; register KubernetesConfiguration service provider; or force delete and reinstall Dapr extension

symptom: Dapr extension install fails: ExtensionOperationFailed - Failed to resolve the extension version from the given values
rootCause: Targeted Dapr version does not exist
solution: Verify and use a supported Dapr version from the official supported versions list

symptom: Dapr extension install fails: ExtensionTypeRegistrationGetFailed - Extension type microsoft.dapr is not registered in region
rootCause: Dapr version not available in the specified Azure region
solution: Install Dapr extension in a region where the target version is supported

symptom: Dapr extension install fails: ServiceAccount dapr-operator in namespace dapr-system exists and cannot be imported - invalid ownership metadata
rootCause: Dapr OSS (open-source) already installed on cluster, conflicts with AKS Dapr extension Helm release
solution: Uninstall Dapr OSS first, then install the Dapr extension. See Migrate from Dapr OSS to Dapr extension guide

symptom: Dapr placement server pod unschedulable: 0/N nodes available, volume node affinity conflict
rootCause: Placement server pod tries to use persistent volume created in a different availability zone
solution: Install Dapr in multiple AZs (recommended), or limit placement service to specific AZ by creating custom StorageClass with allowedTopologies and setting dapr_placement.volumeclaims.storageClassName

symptom: AKS upgrade fails: UpgradeFailed - Evict blocked by conflicting disruption budgets; pod has more than one PodDisruptionBudget
rootCause: Pod covered by multiple PDBs; Kubernetes eviction API does not support evicting pods matched by more than one PDB, blocking node drain
solution: Identify conflicting PDBs via kubectl get pdb -n <ns> -o wide; delete one conflicting PDB so each pod is covered by at most one; reconcile and retry upgrade. Consider using Eviction Autoscaler extension for automatic PDB management

symptom: Kubernetes marketplace offer deployment fails: AuthorizationFailed - does not have authorization to perform extensionaddons/read
rootCause: Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration resource provider not registered on the subscription
solution: Register the Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration resource provider; check AKS cluster health; examine Azure Monitor activity log

symptom: Kubernetes marketplace offer fails: payment instrument not supported; offer cannot be purchased in region; or legal terms not accepted
rootCause: Subscription billing constraints, regional availability restrictions, or legal terms not accepted
solution: Verify subscription billing configuration; check offer regional availability; accept legal terms via Azure portal, CLI, or PowerShell

symptom: Kubernetes marketplace offer: Extension failed to deploy with Internal server error; or Failed to install chart from path for release
rootCause: Extension resource installation failure or incorrect Helm chart values in ARM template
solution: For internal server error: delete extension resource and reinstall. For Helm chart failure: verify values in Azure portal ARM template deployment

symptom: Key Vault Secrets Provider: FailedMount - failed to get keyvault client: nmi response failed with status code 404
rootCause: NMI component in aad-pod-identity returned error for token request; pod identity not properly configured
